summary: The absence of normal blood pressure requiring no medication supports brain stem function. Decerebrate or decorticate postures imply some level of cerebral preservation; thus they cannot confirm complete brain death. Sweating and increased heart rate may be indicators of other conditions like autonomic dysregulation instead of conclusive brain death criteria. Therefore, the most likely exception among provided options is C) sweating and tachycardia as clear markers of brain death.
